entries.title: COMMENTARY ON THE APOCALYPSE 16:16 on revelation 16:16
entries.excerpt: Armageddon is translated as "cutting" or "being thoroughly cut up." And so we should think that the nations gathered and led for battle there by the devil will be slaughtered, since the devil rejoices in human blood.

entries.title: Commentary on Revelation on revelation 16:16
entries.excerpt: And he gathered them together in a place called in Hebrew Armageddon. He explains this place elsewhere, saying: He gathered them for battle, and they surrounded the camp of the saints, and the beloved city (Rev. 20), that is, the Church. Conversely, the place of the wicked can be understood as the devil, who in a lost man, inflated with the pride of usurped divinity, will rejoice in recovering former deceptions, that is, those once forbidden by the Lord. For Armageddon means a return to the former, or a swollen mount.

entries.title: Commentary on Revelation on revelation 16:16
entries.excerpt: And the demons, he says, gathered the kings of the whole inhabited earth into the place called in Hebrew Meggido. This is interpreted as "Cutting off" or "to be cut off." Therefore he called the place of war Meggido from the cutting off and slaughter destined to be gathered there.
